candidateAtoms.subject: ts-notification-service.NotificationService
candidateAtoms.object: four notify-outcome methods (preserveSuccess, orderCreateSuccess, orderChangedSuccess, orderCancelSuccess) each take NotifyInfo plus HttpHeaders and return a boolean success flag

candidateAtoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.SecurityConfig
candidateAtoms.object: edu.fudan.common.security.jwt.JWTFilter added as a pre-authentication filter before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter

candidateAtoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.Order
candidateAtoms.object: equals compares business fields boughtDate, travelDate, travelTime, accountId, contactsName, contactsDocumentNumber, documentType, trainNumber, coachNumber, seatClass, seatNumber, from, to, status, price and excludes id; this equals is used by OrderOtherServiceImpl.create and addNewOrder as the accountOrders.contains duplicate-order check

candidateAtoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderAlterInfo
candidateAtoms.object: carries previousOrderId, newOrderInfo and loginToken to model altering an existing order into a replacement order

candidateAtoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderOtherService
candidateAtoms.object: methods return a Response wrapping data or a not-found/rejected message, except initOrder which returns void; every method accepts an HttpHeaders headers parameter even where some implementations do not read it

candidateAtoms.subject: ts-order-service.SecurityConfig
candidateAtoms.object: edu.fudan.common.security.jwt.JWTFilter added as a pre-authentication filter before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter

candidateCovers.label: ts-order-other-service order CRUD endpoints measured against its authority surface
ts-order-service order endpoints measured against its authority surface
antMatcher declaration-order convention across ts-order-service and ts-order-other-service SecurityConfig

notes: send() logs the value then forwards it to the configured queue via AmqpTemplate.convertAndSend
each of the four notify methods returns false on the branch where mailService.sendEmail throws, rather than propagating the exception
the broad permitAll wildcard antMatcher for orderOther/** is declared ahead of the narrower role-specific POST/PUT/DELETE antMatchers for the same base path
queryOrdersForRefresh reassembles from/to station names by list-index alignment after the queryForStationId provider call, not by an id-keyed lookup

the narrower role-specific POST/PUT/DELETE antMatchers for the order path are declared ahead of the broad permitAll wildcard antMatcher for the same base path -- the opposite declaration order from ts-order-other-service's SecurityConfig
